attacking_style: Late-stage breakaway accelerations within final 3km, capable of sustaining solo efforts when peloton miscalculates closing distance. Opportunistic rather than sustained climber attacks.
team_tactics: Limited team support evident; relies on breakaway positioning and individual strength rather than organized leadout or domestique assistance.
terrain_preference: Stages where breakaways can succeed through peloton misjudgment rather than pure climbing power. Benefits from tactical chaos and organizational gaps in chase.
vulnerability: Requires peloton hesitation or miscalculation to succeed; unlikely to overpower organized chase from strong teams. Previously unemployed status suggests consistency issues.
key_patterns: Executes late solo accelerations from breakaways in final 3km to exploit peloton hesitation
Sustains time gaps around 20-30 seconds against disorganized chase efforts in finale
Career instability with unemployment periods suggests opportunistic rather than consistent performer
form_trajectory: positive: Won from breakaway after unemployment spell, showing ability to capitalize on rare opportunities when they arise
